mission.title: Install and Configure Rook Ceph Operator
mission.description: Rook Ceph Operator is a cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes that automates the deployment and management of Ceph storage. It provides self-managing, self-scaling, and self-healing storage services, making it ideal for applications requiring reliable and scalable storage solutions. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to simpler storage solutions.

steps.title: Add Rook Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Rook Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Rook using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add rook-release https://charts.rook.io/release

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Rook Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Rook Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Rook operator in the 'rook-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace rook-system

helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

```

> Creates a namespace for Rook.
> Installs the Rook Ceph operator.
commands: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

steps.title: Create Rook Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Ceph cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rook/rook/v1.19.3/deploy/examples/cluster.yaml

```

> Deploys the Ceph cluster configuration.

steps.title: Verify Rook Ceph Cluster
steps.description:
This step verifies that the Rook Ceph cluster is running and healthy.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system get pods

kubectl -n rook-system get cephcluster

```

> Check the status of the Rook operator and Ceph pods.
> Verify the Ceph cluster status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Rook Ceph Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Rook operator and may result in data loss. Ensure to backup any data before proceeding.

```bash

helm uninstall rook-ceph --namespace rook-system

kubectl delete namespace rook-system

```

> Uninstalls the Rook Ceph operator.
> Deletes the Rook namespace and all resources within.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Rook Ceph Operator
upgrade.description:
Backup your data, drain the nodes if necessary, upgrade the operator, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl -n rook-system get cephcluster -o yaml > cephcluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

kubectl -n rook-system get pods

```

> Backup the current Ceph cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the Rook Ceph operator.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl -n rook-system get cephcluster -o yaml > cephcluster-backup.yaml
helm upgrade rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create Ceph cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the resource allocation and ensure the configuration in cluster.yaml is correct.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there are issues with the Ceph configuration or insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rook-system' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Ceph cluster status is 'HEALTH_WARN'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are warnings in the Ceph cluster, possibly due to OSDs being down.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl -n rook-system get cephcluster' to check the status and troubleshoot OSDs.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Rook operator is not installed or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Rook operator is installed and check the namespace with 'kubectl get namespaces'.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for CephFS and RBD snapshots, added new metrics for monitoring.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy storage classes.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.18.x, ensure to update your storage class definitions.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl -n rook-system get pods' showing all pods in 'Running' state and 'kubectl -n rook-system get cephcluster' indicating 'HEALTH_OK'.
codeSnippets: helm install rook-ceph rook-release/rook-ceph --namespace rook-system --version v1.19.3

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
